review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls related to transmission confidentiality and integrity. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but minor improvements could enhance clarity and consistency. The functions are technically correct and align with the NIST 800-53 SC-8(1) control.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'enforced' consistently for clarity, e.g., 'k8s_ingress_tls_enforced' instead of 'k8s_ingress_tls_enabled'.
title: Transmission Confidentiality And Integrity - Transmission confidentiality and integrity | cryptographic protection implement
step1_reasoning: The control SC-8(1) focuses on ensuring transmission confidentiality and integrity through cryptographic protection. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring secure communication channels through the use of TLS for network policies, ingress controllers, service mesh, and etcd. These components can enforce cryptographic protocols to protect data in transit, aligning with the control's requirements.
